compressed_prompt:
Write an acoustic song about the Korean peninsula without using any commas.  
Compose an acoustic song on the Korean peninsula theme no commas.
response:
**"Two Rivers, One Sky"**
*(An Acoustic Song About the Korean Peninsula)*

*(Verse 1)*
Beneath the same moon’s silver glow
Two rivers flow where the wild winds blow
One to the east and one to the west
Carrying dreams like a whispered request

*(Pre-Chorus)*
The mountains stand tall through the years
Holding the weight of both joy and tears
A land divided by lines in the sand
But the heart still beats in the same old land

*(Chorus)*
Oh Korea oh my love
Bound by the stars up above
Though the world tries to pull us apart
We’re stitched by the thread of the same old heart

*(Verse 2)*
The children play in the morning light
Laughing and running with all of their might
One in the north and one in the south
Same sunrise same hopeful mouth

*(Pre-Chorus)*
The elders tell stories of days long gone
Of a time when the land was one
Now borders rise like a wall so high
But the roots run deep where the rivers lie

*(Chorus)*
Oh Korea oh my love
Bound by the stars up above
Though the world tries to pull us apart
We’re stitched by the thread of the same old heart

*(Bridge)*
One day the tide will turn once more
The waves will crash on the same old shore
No more goodbyes no more pain
Just the dawn breaking after the rain

*(Outro)*
Beneath the same moon’s silver glow
Two rivers flow where the wild winds blow
One to the east and one to the west
Carrying hope like a sacred quest

---
*Style Notes:*
- **Melody:** Gentle fingerpicking (like *Nick Drake* or *Iron & Wine*), soft strumming in the chorus.
- **Theme:** Focus on unity, shared history, and quiet resilience—no political blame, just longing.
- **Instrumentation:** Acoustic guitar, light harmonica or strings for texture.

Would you like any adjustments to the mood or imagery?
